steps.action: Kpanlogo is a recreational dance and music form originating from the 1960s among urban youth in Accra, Ghana.
steps.sourceSnippet: Kpanlogo is a recreational dance and music form originating from the 1960s among urban youth in Accra, Ghana.

steps.action: It began in the early 1960s as an innovative dance form, influenced by American rock and roll, and giving the younger Ga generations a point of distinction from their elders.
steps.sourceSnippet: It began in the early 1960s as an innovative dance form, influenced by American rock and roll, and giving the younger Ga generations a point of distinction from their elders.

steps.action: The kpanlogo dance is often performed low to the ground, with bent knees and bent back, and frequently features sexually suggestive motions.
steps.sourceSnippet: The kpanlogo dance is often performed low to the ground, with bent knees and bent back, and frequently features sexually suggestive motions.

steps.action: The music accompanying the kpanlogo dance is drawn from older Ga drumming traditions, such as gome, oge and kolomashie.
steps.sourceSnippet: The music accompanying the kpanlogo dance is drawn from older Ga drumming traditions, such as gome, oge and kolomashie.

steps.action: It is also similar to the "Bo Diddley beat" popularized by the U.
steps.sourceSnippet: It is also similar to the "Bo Diddley beat" popularized by the U.

steps.action: "Kpanlogo: A Detailed Description of One Arrangement of a West-African Music and Dance Genre.
steps.sourceSnippet: "Kpanlogo:  A Detailed Description of One Arrangement of a West-African Music and Dance Genre.

steps.action: Accounts of police seizing musical instruments and detaining performers in its early days have been documented.
steps.sourceSnippet: Accounts of police seizing musical instruments and detaining performers in its early days have been documented.

steps.action: Kpanlogo music uses three types of instruments: nono (metal bell), fao (gourd rattle), and kpanlogo drums.
steps.sourceSnippet: Kpanlogo music uses three types of instruments: nono (metal bell), fao (gourd rattle), and kpanlogo drums.

steps.action: Nono plays the key pattern or timeline of the music, supported by the fao.
steps.sourceSnippet: Nono plays the key pattern or timeline of the music, supported by the fao.

steps.action: It is common to have three kpanlogo drums in an ensemble, in the roles of "male voice", "female voice" and "master drum".
steps.sourceSnippet: It is common to have three kpanlogo drums in an ensemble, in the roles of "male voice", "female voice" and "master drum".

steps.action: The main kpanlogo bell part is one of the most common and oldest key patterns found in sub-Saharan Africa.
steps.sourceSnippet: The main kpanlogo bell part is one of the most common and oldest key patterns found in sub-Saharan Africa.

steps.action: The bell pattern used in kpanlogo is the same as the son clave pattern heard in Cuban music and.
steps.sourceSnippet: The bell pattern used in kpanlogo is the same as the son clave pattern heard in Cuban music and.
